description: The distributive quantifier that singles out every member of a set individually. Where 'theula' gathers all into wholeness, 'theli' honors each separately—the same scope but different focus. It is the grammar of individual attention within collective membership, ensuring that universal statements touch each particular being rather than dissolving them into abstraction.
sound_symbolism: The word begins with 'th' /θ/, the thoughtful interdental fricative that requires careful tongue placement—the sound of deliberate, individual attention. This measured opening flows into 'e', the balanced mid vowel that neither rushes nor lingers. Finally, 'li' reaches outward with liquid 'l' toward the focused 'i', as if touching each point in turn. Speaking 'theli' enacts the distributive gesture: careful consideration reaching to each individual. The shared 'the' with 'theula' (all) marks them as siblings—both universal but one collective, one distributive.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a distributive quantifier, emphasizing individual members rather than collective totality. Precedes nouns: 'theli himo' (each person), 'theli nila' (each day). Often implies that what is predicated holds separately for each: 'theli himo wela na hisu' (each person individually deserves to feel peace). Distinguished from 'theula' (all) which views the set as a whole. Can combine with singular or plural nouns depending on whether the individual or the distributed set is emphasized.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Compassion must reach each being, not just beings in general. 'Theli' grammaticalizes the bodhisattva's vow to liberate every sentient being—not as undifferentiated mass but as countless individuals, each deserving full attention.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Justice requires seeing each person, not just categories of people. 'Theli' supports this ethical attention, ensuring that universal principles are applied with individual care rather than bureaucratic abstraction.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Traditional crafts teach attention to each stitch, each seed, each student. 'Theli' carries this understanding that quality emerges from care given to every individual instance, not just to processes in general.
